old_claim.note: The proving ground was a dedicated weapons-testing facility with proof batteries and ranges for systematic ordnance tests.

new_claim_absent: Sandy Hook was established in 1874 as its own specialist proving ground, twenty-six years before the separately scoped NPL national standards laboratory.

source_shape.source_locator: NPS says the Army established Sandy Hook in 1874 as a dedicated proving ground for systematic tests of new weapons, guns, gunpowder, shells, fuses, and primers.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: NPS documents Sandy Hook as a dedicated U.S. Army weapons-testing site established in 1874.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Its own proof batteries and ranges supplied the testing institution; later national metrology laboratories are a parallel class rather than an ancestor.

ontology_before: industrial_ballistics_laboratories inherited directly from industrial_laboratories_standard_testing as an origin or component dependency.
ontology_after: industrial_ballistics_laboratories no longer inherits from industrial_laboratories_standard_testing; the source-pinned scope is represented by its remaining prerequisites.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: industrial_ballistics_laboratories no longer has a direct prerequisite edge from industrial_laboratories_standard_testing.
would_reject_if: Direct historical evidence showed that industrial_laboratories_standard_testing was an operational prerequisite for the source-pinned industrial_ballistics_laboratories milestone rather than later context or a parallel practice.
short_reason: The 1874 proving ground cannot depend on a 1900 national laboratory model.
